ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
ffmpeg:
- '7'
fmt:
- '12.1'
freetype:
- '2'
giflib:
- '5.2'
imath:
- 3.2.2
libboost_devel:
- '1.88'
libheif:
- '1.19'
libjpeg_turbo:
- '3'
libpng:
- '1.6'
libraw:
- '0.21'
libtiff:
- '4.7'
libwebp:
- '1'
numpy:
- '2'
openexr:
- '3.4'
openjpeg:
- '2'
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
- 3.11.* *_cpython
- 3.12.* *_cpython
- 3.13.* *_cp313
- 3.14.* *_cp314
target_platform:
- linux-64
tbb_devel:
- '2022'
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
zlib:
- '1'

about.description:
OpenImageIO consists of:
- Simple but powerful ImageInput and ImageOutput APIs that provide an abstraction
  for reading and writing image files of nearly any format, without the calling
  application needing to know any of the details of these file formats, and indeed
  without the calling application needing to be aware of which formats are available.
- Plugins implementing I/O for several popular image file formats, including TIFF,
  JPEG/JFIF, OpenEXR, PNG, HDR/RGBE, ICO, BMP, Targa, JPEG-2000, RMan Zfile, FITS, DDS,
  Softimage PIC, PNM, DPX, Cineon, IFF, Field3D, Ptex, Photoshop PSD, Wavefront RLA, SGI,
  WebP, GIF, DICOM, many \"RAW\" digital camera formats, and a variety of movie formats
  (readable as individual frames). More are being developed all the time.
- An ImageCache class that transparently manages a cache so that it can access truly
  vast amounts of image data (tens of thousands of image files totaling multiple TB)
  very efficiently using only a tiny amount (tens of megabytes at most) of runtime memory.
about.summary: Simple but powerful APIs for reading & writing many image formats

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  version: "2.5.18.0"
  build_number: 12

recipe:
  name: openimageio-split
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/OpenImageIO/oiio/archive/refs/tags/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: f57481435cec18633d3eba9b2e8c483fc1df6f0a01c5c9f98cbae6d1c52928e5

build:
  number: ${{ build_number }}

outputs:
  - package:
      name: openimageio
    requirements:
      build:
        - ${{ compiler("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - cmake
        - ninja
      host:
        - ffmpeg
        - fmt
        - freetype
        - giflib
        - imath
        - libboost-devel
        - libheif
        - libjpeg-turbo
        - libpng
        - libraw
        - libtiff
        - libwebp
        - openexr
        - openjpeg
        - tbb-devel
        - tsl_robin_map
        - zlib
      ignore_run_exports:
        by_name:
          - fmt
      run_exports:
        strong:
          - ${{ pin_subpackage("openimageio", upper_bound="x.x") }}
    build:
      number: ${{ build_number }}
      script:
        env:
          OIIO_BUILD_PYTHON: "0"
        file: build
    tests:
      - package_contents:
          include:
            - OpenImageIO/oiioversion.h
          lib:
            - OpenImageIO
            - OpenImageIO_Util
          files:
            - if: not win
              then:
                - lib/cmake/OpenImageIO/OpenImageIOConfig.cmake
                - lib/cmake/OpenImageIO/OpenImageIOTargets.cmake
                - lib/pkgconfig/OpenImageIO.pc
                - share/fonts/OpenImageIO/DroidSans.ttf
            - if: win
              then:
                - Library/lib/cmake/OpenImageIO/OpenImageIOConfig.cmake
                - Library/lib/cmake/OpenImageIO/OpenImageIOTargets.cmake

  - package:
      name: py-openimageio
    requirements:
      build:
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then:
            - python
            - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
            - pybind11
            - numpy
        - ${{ compiler("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - cmake
        - ninja
      host:
        - python
        - numpy
        - pybind11
        - ffmpeg
        - fmt
        - freetype
        - giflib
        - imath
        - libboost-devel
        - libheif
        - libjpeg-turbo
        - libpng
        - libraw
        - libtiff
        - libwebp
        - openexr
        - openjpeg
        - tbb-devel
        - tsl_robin_map
        - zlib
      
      # The build script rebuilds the library (so same host deps need to be present),
      # but the python bits don't need them (and we run-depend on openimageio here)
      ignore_run_exports:
        by_name:
          - ffmpeg
          - fmt
          - freetype
          - giflib
          - imath
          - libboost-devel
          - libheif
          - libjpeg-turbo
          - libpng
          - libraw
          - libtiff
          - libwebp
          - numpy
          - openexr
          - openjpeg
          - tbb-devel
          - zlib
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("openimageio", exact=True) }}
        - python
    build:
      number: ${{ build_number }}
      string: py${{ python | version_to_buildstring }}_h${{ hash }}_${{ build_number }}
      script:
        env:
          OIIO_BUILD_PYTHON: "1"
        file: build
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - OpenImageIO

about:
  homepage: https://sites.google.com/site/openimageio/home
  license: BSD-3-Clause
  license_file: LICENSE.md
  summary: Simple but powerful APIs for reading & writing many image formats
  description: |
    OpenImageIO consists of:
    - Simple but powerful ImageInput and ImageOutput APIs that provide an abstraction
      for reading and writing image files of nearly any format, without the calling
      application needing to know any of the details of these file formats, and indeed
      without the calling application needing to be aware of which formats are available.
    - Plugins implementing I/O for several popular image file formats, including TIFF,
      JPEG/JFIF, OpenEXR, PNG, HDR/RGBE, ICO, BMP, Targa, JPEG-2000, RMan Zfile, FITS, DDS,
      Softimage PIC, PNM, DPX, Cineon, IFF, Field3D, Ptex, Photoshop PSD, Wavefront RLA, SGI,
      WebP, GIF, DICOM, many \"RAW\" digital camera formats, and a variety of movie formats
      (readable as individual frames). More are being developed all the time.
    - An ImageCache class that transparently manages a cache so that it can access truly
      vast amounts of image data (tens of thousands of image files totaling multiple TB)
      very efficiently using only a tiny amount (tens of megabytes at most) of runtime memory.
  documentation: https://openimageio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
  repository: https://github.com/OpenImageIO/oiio

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - SylvainCorlay
    - JohanMabille
    - wolfv
    - lgritz
    - h-vetinari
  feedstock-name: openimageio
